channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: From Singapore to Brussels, world leaders eye Trump-Xi summitВ¬вЂ from afar
item.description: The world will be watching the Trump-Xi summit.

item.title: Stock futures slide as investors monitor the latest Iran war negotiations: Live updates
item.description: The three major indexes are coming off a winning weak.

item.title: Iran says it will 'never bow' as Trump rejects peace counteroffer, prolonging Middle East conflict
item.description: Washington has sought to press Beijing to lean on Tehran to reopen the strait, though China's appetite to act as a pressure mechanism remains unclear.

item.title: Modi says Iran war poses severe risks to India, urges cuts in fuel use and gold purchases
item.description: Modi appealed to Indians to cut fuel use, overseas travel and gold purchases as the Middle East conflict exposes IndiaвЂљГ„Гґs heavy reliance on imported energy.

item.title: Brent oil tops $105 after Trump dismisses IranвЂљГ„Гґs peace proposal response
item.description: Oil prices jumped on Monday after Israel warned that the conflict with Iran was still ongoing.

item.title: Memory chip makers are looking at a 'supercycle' and 'windfall gains.' The stocks jumped 30% in one week
item.description: Higher prices on chips are boosting margin projections through 2027.

item.title: Singapore flags weaker tourism spending as global travel industry faces uncertainty
item.description: Singapore expects tourism spending to ease from last yearвЂљГ„Гґs record, even as visitor arrivals continue to rise.

item.title: European markets mixed as Iran's peace talks stall, defense stocks slide
item.description: European stocks were largely mixed in Monday trade as investors digest the latest in U.S.-Iran peace negotiations.

item.title: China inflation beat estimates in April as Iran war drives producer prices to three-year highs
item.description: China has cushioned the worst of the energy shock through its strategic oil stockpiles and a diversified mix of renewable energy sources.

item.title: Bank of America says stocks like Apple have plenty of upside following earnings
item.description: The firm named five stocks that have more room to run following earnings.

item.title: Why one of the nation's largest auto lenders isn't worried about high vehicle prices or 'forever loans'
item.description: While median car payments have jumped from $390 to $525 since 2019, data provided by Capital One suggests stability in vehicle cost compared to income.

item.title: Used car prices fall for the first time this year and EV interest rises as gas prices spike
item.description: Cox Automotive reports affordability remains a key concern for buyers and that's driving increased demand for older vehicles as well as all-electric vehicles.

item.title: South Korea's Kospi hits fresh record as Asia markets trade mixed amid oil surge, Iran risks
item.description: Investors headed into Monday on edge as TrumpвЂљГ„Гґs rejection of IranвЂљГ„Гґs ceasefire proposal fueled concerns about a prolonged Middle East conflict.

item.title: Netanyahu says Iran war is 'not over' as Trump rejects latest Iranian offer
item.description: The Iran war, dragging into its second month, has spiked oil and gas prices in the U.S. and around the world.

item.title: Do you need a chief AI officer? Here's how the tech is changing boardrooms
item.description: AI may now be coming for the C-suite, according to a report published Monday by IBM, which found that most companies were now staffing chief AI officer roles.

item.title: Meet the YouTube whisperers, a booming class of advisors behind MrBeast and other million-dollar channels
item.description: A burgeoning group of YouTube strategists are charging big money to YouTubers who want to expand their popularity.

item.title: Michael Burry says the market today feels like 'the last months of the 1999-2000 bubble'
item.description: "Stocks are not up or down because of jobs or consumer sentiment," Burry wrote. "Feeling like the last months of the 1999-2000 bubble."

item.title: Cerebras to raise IPO price range to $150 to $160 a share as demand surges, sources sayвЂљГ„ДЌвЂљГ„ДЌвЂљГ„ДЌвЂљГ„ДЌвЂљГ„ДЌвЂљГ„ДЌвЂљГ„ДЌвЂљГ„ДЌвЂљГ„ДЌвЂљГ„ДЌвЂљГ„ДЌвЂљГ„ДЌвЂљГ„ДЌвЂљГ„ДЌвЂљГ„ДЌвЂљГ„ДЌ
item.description: Cerebras is considering an IPO price range of $150 to $160 a share and raising the number of shares marketed to 30 million from 28 million, Reuters reported.

item.title: Why pension funds are doubling down on private credit despite deepening cracks
item.description: Pension funds are sticking with private credit, and in some cases doubling down even as concerns mount over underwriting standards, valuation opacity and sector concentration.

item.title: It's not too late to start buying the data center winners. Here's why
item.description: In his Sunday column for Investing Club subscribers, Jim Cramer pushes back on the idea that it's too late to start buying AI winners.

item.title: With Netflix new ad-free standard plan at $20, streaming's tipping point into old TV is getting closer
item.description: The economics of streaming are nearing the point where cheaper plans with ads make as much if not more money for services like Netflix.

item.title: Amazon Prime Day 2026 is in June. Get a $150 Amazon gift card and extra rewards with the right credit card
item.description: Amazon Prime Day is coming early this year; it's time to prepare. We explain when the sale is actually worth it and the right cards to use for added perks.

item.title: Alphabet's 160% rally in a year reflects value of owning 'most of the stack' in AI
item.description: Google was seen as an AI laggard in the early days of the AI boom, but investors are now betting that the search giant will be a clear winner.

item.title: Retailers are on a hiring spree. But consumers are sending warning signs
item.description: The retail trades added nearly 22,000 jobs in April, accounting for about one-fifth of total job growth.

item.title: Target is trying to win back busy families from Walmart, starting with the baby aisle
item.description: Target is refreshing its baby category as part of a broader effort to offer better products and more convenience to compete with Walmart and Amazon.

item.title: Evacuation flights leave Tenerife after cruise ship virus outbreak
item.description: Eight people on board fell ill, including three who died. Hantavirus is usually spread by rodents but can in rare cases be transmitted person-to-person.

item.title: SEC delay on prediction markets ETFs echoes a long-fought bitcoin fund battle
item.description: The Securities and Exchange Commission has delayed approval for prediction markets ETFs, bringing up memories of the long battle over bitcoin funds.

item.title: Saudi Aramco first-quarter profit jumps 26% as key pipeline reaches capacity amid Iran war
item.description: Aramco says its East-West pipeline has helped to mitigate the energy shock caused by the Iran war.

item.title: Frontier jet hits and kills pedestrian on runway in Denver during takeoff
item.description: Frontier said that smoke was reported in the cabin of the Airbus A321 and the pilots aborted takeoff.

item.title: MV Hondius hantavirus outbreak: A luxury expedition cruise boom and new world of remote travel risks
item.description: Remote expedition cruises to destinations like Antarctica and the Arctic are booming, but the deadly hantavirus aboard MV Hondius is a reminder of unique risks.
